proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months on enzalutamide=3.728 vs off=3.755 (Welch t-test).
Mean pfs_months on abiraterone=3.740 vs off=3.746.
Mean pfs_months on docetaxel=3.732 vs off=3.749.
Mean pfs_months on olaparib=3.829 vs off=3.735; ~0.094 month higher on olaparib.
Mean pfs_months on Lu-177-PSMA=3.741 vs off=3.745.
Mean pfs_months on pembrolizumab=3.805 vs off=3.741; trend toward higher PFS but n.s.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have lower mean pfs_months than mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than visceral_mets=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have different mean pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have lower mean pfs_months than ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have different mean pfs_months than msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have different mean pfs_months than psma_high=0.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than liver_mets=0.
Patients with bone_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than bone_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months mCRPC=3.51 vs non-mCRPC=4.03; very large negative effect (~-0.52 months).
Mean pfs_months visceral=3.706 vs no-visceral=3.754; small negative effect.
Mean pfs_months BRCA2-mut=3.835 vs wild-type=3.734; +0.10 months in BRCA2-mut (likely driven by olaparib treatment subset).
Mean pfs_months AR-V7+=3.734 vs AR-V7−=3.747; no main-effect difference.
Mean pfs_months MSI-high=3.778 vs MSS=3.743; not significant.
Mean pfs_months PSMA-high=3.752 vs PSMA-low=3.732; not significant.
Mean pfs_months liver-mets=3.743 vs none=3.744; no effect.
Mean pfs_months bone-mets=3.753 vs none=3.742; no effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher gleason_score is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope) — usual prior expectation.

analyses.result_summary: β=-1.16 months per ECOG unit; very strong negative.
β=-0.0033 months per ng/mL PSA; significant negative.
β=+0.495 months per g/dL albumin; significant positive.
β=-0.00042 per U/L LDH; significant negative.
β=-0.0749 per % weight loss; strong negative.
β=-0.0062 per Gleason point; not significant unadjusted.
β=-0.000497 per U/L; small but significant negative.
β=+0.174 months per year of age; significantly POSITIVE — the opposite of the expected prior. Suggests cohort/data structural effect rather than a true biologic age benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than brca2_mutation=0 (positive treatment×BRCA2 interaction).
treatment_lu177_psma improves pfs_months more in patients with psma_high=1 than psma_high=0 (positive treatment×PSMA-high interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in patients with msi_high=1 than msi_high=0 (positive treatment×MSI-high interaction).
treatment_enzalutamide is less effective for pfs_months in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 than ar_v7_positive=0 (negative treatment×AR-V7 interaction).
treatment_abiraterone is less effective for pfs_months in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 than ar_v7_positive=0 (negative treatment×AR-V7 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Strongly positive interaction. In BRCA2-mut: olaparib raises mean PFS by +1.55 months (5.22 vs 3.67). In BRCA2-WT: −0.07 (no benefit). Interaction β=+1.62, p=4.3e-61. STRONG PREDICTIVE BIOMARKER signal.
No interaction: effect of Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-high=−0.011 vs PSMA-low=+0.006 months. Interaction β=−0.017 (p=0.74). Contradicts the biological prior.
No interaction: pembrolizumab effect in MSI-high=−0.27 vs MSS=+0.07 months (interaction β=−0.34, p=0.15). Trend is opposite to the immunotherapy prior; not significant.
No interaction: enzalutamide effect AR-V7+=−0.024, AR-V7−=−0.027 (interaction β=+0.002, p=0.96). Contradicts AR-V7 resistance prior.
No interaction: abiraterone effect AR-V7+=+0.032, AR-V7−=−0.015 (interaction β=+0.048, p=0.33). Direction opposite to AR-V7 resistance prior; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction on pfs_months remains positive and significant after adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, psa_ng_ml, weight_loss_pct_6mo.
The olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction on pfs_months is observed within the mcrpc=1 subgroup.
The olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction on pfs_months is observed within the mcrpc=0 subgroup as well.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ olaparib*brca2 + age + ecog + mcrpc + albumin + ldh + psa + weight_loss
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction β=+1.44 months, p=2.4e-960 (effectively 0). Olaparib's BRCA2-conditional benefit is robust to clinical covariate adjustment.
Within mCRPC: interaction β=+1.63 months, p=5.6e-36. Olaparib raises PFS by ~1.6 months in BRCA2-mutated mCRPC; no benefit in BRCA2-WT mCRPC.
Within non-mCRPC: interaction β=+1.63 months, p=1.3e-28. The same predictive effect is present in non-castration-resistant disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS for pfs_months including treatments, biomarkers, labs, demographics, and metastatic sites, treatment_olaparib has an independent positive coefficient.
In the same multivariable model, mcrpc has an independent negative coefficient.
In the same multivariable model, ecog_ps has an independent negative coefficient.
In the same multivariable model, albumin_g_dl has an independent positive coefficient.
In the same multivariable model, age_years has a positive coefficient (refining the surprising univariate finding).
In the multivariable model, brca2_mutation by itself (no interaction) has a positive coefficient — indicating measurable benefit even without olaparib in this cohort.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ age + ecog + mcrpc + visceral + liver + bone + psa + gleason + brca2 + arv7 + msi + psma + albumin + ldh + crp + nlr + hgb + weight_loss + lines + 6 treatments + pain + fatigue
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable model R² = 0.948. Independent predictors: ecog_ps β=-1.152 (p<1e-300); mcrpc β=-0.375 (p<1e-300); age_years β=+0.174 (p<1e-300, positive); albumin_g_dl β=+0.478 (p<1e-300); psa_ng_ml β=-0.0027; weight_loss_pct_6mo β=-0.077; gleason_score β=-0.011 (p<1e-7, becomes sig with adjustment); ldh β=-0.0005; treatment_olaparib β=+0.146 (p=1.9e-100, independent positive); brca2_mutation β=+0.154 (p=4.4e-110); psma_high β=+0.022; visceral_mets β=-0.021. Other treatments not independently significant. Pembrolizumab trend β=+0.017 (p=0.075).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Black patients (race_ethnicity='black') have shorter pfs_months than white patients.
Hispanic patients have shorter pfs_months than white patients.
Asian patients have different pfs_months than white patients.
Medicaid-insured patients have shorter pfs_months than privately insured patients.
Uninsured patients have shorter pfs_months than privately insured patients.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have different pfs_months than urban patients.

analyses.result_summary: Black=3.741 vs White=3.741; difference −0.0005 months (n.s.). No racial disparity in PFS.
Hispanic=3.737 vs White=3.741; n.s.
Asian=3.797 vs White=3.741; +0.056 months, n.s.
Medicaid=3.741 vs Private=3.736; n.s.
Uninsured=3.726 vs Private=3.736; n.s.
Rural=3.756 vs urban=3.741; n.s.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with diabetes_mellitus=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with chronic_kidney_disease=1 have different pfs_months than those without.
Patients with heart_failure=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with copd=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with hypertension=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: Diabetic=3.722 vs no=3.750; n.s.
COPD=3.758 vs no=3.739; n.s.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, ldh_u_l, albumin_g_dl, psa_ng_ml, gleason_score, treatment_olaparib has a positive independent effect on pfs_months.
After the same adjustment, treatment_enzalutamide has no independent effect on pfs_months.
After the same adjustment, treatment_abiraterone has no independent effect on pfs_months.
After the same adjustment, treatment_docetaxel has no independent effect on pfs_months.
After the same adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma has no independent effect on pfs_months.
After the same adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ olaparib + age + ecog + mcrpc + visceral + ldh + albumin + psa + gleason
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β=+0.138 months on olaparib (p=1.9e-64). Independent positive effect (driven mostly by BRCA2-mut subgroup).
Adjusted β=-0.0063 (p=0.21); no independent benefit.
Adjusted β=-0.0034 (p=0.53); no independent benefit.
Adjusted β=-0.0056 (p=0.30); no independent benefit.
Adjusted β=-0.0088 (p=0.21); no independent benefit.
Adjusted β=+0.0088 (p=0.45); no independent benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 25 candidate SNPs, at least one is significantly associated with pfs_months at the per-test α=0.05 level (top hit: snp_rs4986893).
After Bonferroni correction across the 25 SNPs (α=0.002), no SNP is significantly associated with pfs_months.
The unadjusted SNP signal at snp_rs4986893 disappears after adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl.

analyses.code: for s in snps: ttest_ind(pfs by s)
analyses.result_summary: Top SNP snp_rs4986893: diff=-0.092 months, unadjusted p=0.015. Five SNPs nominally p<0.25; one nominally p<0.05.
Min p across 25 SNPs is 0.015, which exceeds the Bonferroni-corrected threshold of 0.002. Therefore no SNP survives multiple-testing correction.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ snp_rs4986893 + age + ecog + mcrpc + albumin
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, snp_rs4986893 β=+0.005 (p=0.68). Univariate signal disappears — confounded by clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
A composite symptom_score (fatigue + pain/2 + dyspnea + appetite_loss) is associated with shorter pfs_months even after adjusting for age and ecog_ps.

analyses.result_summary: β=-0.0009 per pain unit; n.s.
β=+0.0036 per grade; n.s.
β=-0.0019 per grade; n.s.
β=-0.0009 per grade; n.s.
Adjusted β=+0.0008 per composite point; n.s. Symptom grades carry no PFS information beyond ECOG/age in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months in ECOG 0–1 (fit) patients.
treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months in ECOG ≥2 (unfit) patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in ECOG 0–1 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in ECOG ≥2 patients.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0–1: olaparib effect = +0.089 months (p=0.009). Significant in fit patients.
ECOG ≥2: olaparib effect = +0.148 months (p=0.044). Modest sig benefit even in unfit patients.
ECOG 0–1: pembrolizumab effect = +0.049 (p=0.28). n.s.
ECOG ≥2: pembrolizumab effect = -0.043 (p=0.69). n.s.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, those receiving any AR-targeting agent (treatment_enzalutamide=1 OR treatment_abiraterone=1) have higher pfs_months than those receiving neither.
treatment_enzalutamide × mcrpc interaction is significant (more or less benefit in mCRPC).
treatment_abiraterone × mcrpc interaction is significant.
treatment_docetaxel × mcrpc interaction is significant.

analyses.result_summary: Within mCRPC: any AR-targeting=3.487 vs none=3.540 months (diff=-0.053, p=0.032). AR-targeting agents are nominally associated with SLIGHTLY SHORTER PFS in mCRPC, opposite of the textbook prior (likely confounding by indication).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ enzalutamide*mcrpc + age + ecog + albumin
analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=-0.0097 (p=0.38). No effect modification.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ abiraterone*mcrpc + age + ecog + albumin
analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=-0.0016 (p=0.90). No effect modification.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ docetaxel*mcrpc + age + ecog + albumin
analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=+0.0096 (p=0.42). No effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs between Black and non-Black patients (treatment×race_black interaction).
The benefit of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs between privately insured and other insurance categories (treatment×private_insurance interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=-0.020 (p=0.53). Black and non-Black patients derive similar olaparib benefit. No racial disparity in treatment effect.

Interaction β=-0.044 (p=0.038). Slightly smaller olaparib benefit in privately insured vs others, but the absolute difference is tiny relative to the +1.55 mo BRCA2-mut effect; interpret cautiously given multiple testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with albumin_g_dl < 3.5 (clinical hypoalbuminemia threshold) have shorter pfs_months than those with normal albumin.
After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, visceral_mets retains a negative association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Hypoalbuminemia: 3.41 vs normal 3.85 months; diff=-0.44 (p=3.2e-97). Strong prognostic feature.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ visceral + age + ecog + mcrpc + albumin
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted visceral β=-0.023 (p=0.0006). Remains a small but statistically robust independent risk factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusting for age, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, brca2_mutation has an independent positive association with pfs_months (i.e., not entirely explained by olaparib confounding).
After adjusting for age, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss, treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months is positive (i.e., MSI-high benefits more).
After the same adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma × psma_high interaction on pfs_months is positive (i.e., PSMA-high benefits more).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ brca2 + age + ecog + mcrpc + albumin + ldh
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted brca2 β=+0.140 (p=8.0e-55). BRCA2-mut patients have higher PFS independent of clinical covariates — and this is partly mediated by olaparib treatment.
Adjusted interaction β=+0.039 (p=0.53). Trend is now slightly positive but firmly non-significant after adjustment. No predictive biomarker signal for pembrolizumab × MSI-high.
Adjusted interaction β=-0.008 (p=0.55). No predictive biomarker signal for Lu-177-PSMA × PSMA-high.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib × treatment_pembrolizumab interaction on pfs_months is positive (synergistic combination).
treatment_olaparib × treatment_lu177_psma interaction on pfs_months is positive.
treatment_olaparib × treatment_docetaxel interaction is significant.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=0.0775, p=0.0654.
Interaction β=0.0169, p=0.4946.
Interaction β=-0.0476, p=0.0136.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The cohort is exclusively male (sex_female=0 for all patients) — sanity check that this is a prostate-only cohort.
psma_high has an independent positive association with pfs_months after adjusting for ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l (refining iter-2 unadjusted result).

analyses.result_summary: All 50,000 patients have sex_female=0. Cohort is entirely male, consistent with prostate cancer.
Adjusted psma_high β=0.0177, p=0.0013. Small but significant independent positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months differs by age_years (treatment×age interaction).
Within BRCA2-mutated patients only, treatment_olaparib has a strong positive effect on pfs_months (refining iter-4).

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib×age β=-0.0005, p=0.5895.
In BRCA2-mut subgroup (n=4996): olaparib=5.22 vs no-olaparib=3.67 months; diff=+1.545 (p=3.95e-27). Confirms strong PARP-inhibitor benefit limited to BRCA2-mutated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The single largest treatment-related effect on pfs_months in this cohort is the olaparib×brca2_mutation interaction (~+1.55 months in BRCA2-mut patients on olaparib vs not).
Established prognostic markers (ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, age_years, psa_ng_ml) explain the vast majority of pfs_months variance (R² ≈ 0.95 in multivariable model).
No SDOH variable (race_ethnicity, insurance_type, rural_residence) is independently associated with pfs_months in this cohort.

Predictive-biomarker effects expected from external priors — pembrolizumab×MSI-high and Lu-177-PSMA×PSMA-high — are NOT observed in this dataset (interactions are non-significant; in the case of pembrolizumab×MSI, even the trend reverses).

analyses.code: ttest BRCA2 olaparib effect vs BRCA2 non-olaparib
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-mut+olaparib subgroup PFS=5.22 mo vs BRCA2-mut alone=3.67 mo; +1.55 mo benefit, p<<1e-30.
Multivariable model R² = 0.948. Clinical features alone capture nearly all reproducible PFS variation.
race_ethnicity, insurance_type, rural_residence: no significant differences in PFS (all p>0.14).

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction tests for pembro×msi and lu177×psma
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab×MSI-high adjusted interaction β=+0.04 (p=0.53); Lu-177-PSMA×PSMA-high adjusted β=-0.008 (p=0.55). Neither significant; predictive-biomarker priors NOT supported by these data.
